evidence_quotes.quote: Please make sure the Digital Fairness Act protects customers from losing access to video games they've bought.
People should be able to keep playing the games they paid for, even if the publisher removes them or shuts down servers.
We need laws that require publishers to create some kind of end-of-life plan to keep games playable in a reasonable state or give offline options after the video game has been sunset.

paragraphs.text: Please make sure the Digital Fairness Act protects customers from losing access to video games they've bought. In publicly compiled database at Stop Killing Games wiki, hundreds of games have already been destroyed, and many more are at risk of disappearing. This not only undermines consumer rights but also erodes cultural preservation and trust in digital markets. The Stop Destroying Videogames European Citizens' Initiative is calling for rules to stop this. People should be able to keep playing the games they paid for, even if the publisher removes them or shuts down servers. We need laws that require publishers to create some kind of end-of-life plan to keep games playable in a reasonable state or give offline options after the video game has been sunset. Thank you for considering this.
